fundamentals.name: Roundhill Memory ETF
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: The fund will generally seek to invest primarily in the equity securities of “Memory Companies,” but may also seek exposure to Memory Companies through derivative instruments, such as swap agreements and forward contracts. Under normal circumstances, it invests at least 80% of its net assets (plus borrowings for investment purposes) in equity securities or instruments (i.e., swap agreements or forward contracts) that provide exposure to Memory Companies. The fund is non-diversified.

news.paragraph: The Roundhill Memory ETF (DRAM) is experiencing significant attention due to a perceived memory "melt-up" fueled by the AI boom, with related stocks like Micron, SanDisk, and Western Digital seeing substantial gains. Market commentators are highlighting DRAM as potentially the fastest-growing ETF in history, though some question if this is merely a momentum trade. The ETF's valuation metrics are not readily available, but the broader sector sentiment appears strongly bullish on memory demand.

headlines.title: Trading at 7x Earnings in an AI Boom? This Is the ‘Stupid Cheap’ Stock Your Financial Advisor Won’t Tell You About
Micron Jumps on Memory Demand Buzz
Not Just Micron: Memory Melt-Up Pulls SanDisk Up 8%, Western Digital Up 10%
AI Memory Boom Powers This Fund To The 'Fastest-Growing ETF' In History, Says Market Commentator, Bitcoin ETF's Record Broken
DRAM: Is the fastest-growing ETF ever just another momentum trade?
